edges.source: ER stress and unfolded protein response

intermediate_mechanisms: STING-dependent type I interferon signaling in lung tissue
Autoimmune pulmonary capillaritis and alveolar hemorrhage
Proinflammatory cytokine activation of synovial inflammation
TH17-skewed adaptive immune activation and B-cell autoantibody production

edges.target: STING accumulation and activation at the Golgi

nodes.description: Inflammatory joint disease is a common extrapulmonary manifestation and can precede or accompany lung disease.

evidence.reference_title: Insights from a novel monogenic autoinflammatory disease: overview of a multicentric European cohort of 38 patients with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-six patients demonstrated joint involvement, and 7 had documented kidney disease.

nodes.description: Convergent type I interferon, NF-kappaB, and TH17/autoantibody output targets the lung (interstitial pneumonia, capillaritis, alveolar haemorrhage, lymphoid hyperplasia), the synovium (inflammatory arthritis), and the kidney (ANCA-associated, lupus-like or immune-complex glomerulonephritis), and is the proximate driver of the clinical phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Imaging findings of COPA Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent imaging finding of COPA syndrome is diffuse lung disease related to early childhood-onset recurrent pulmonary hemorrhage and lymphoid hyperplasia that may progress to pulmonary fibrosis.

evidence.reference_title: Kidney Transplant Outcomes in Coatomer Protein Complex Subunit Alpha (COPA) Syndrome: Report of Five Patients.
evidence.snippet: Kidney involvement was heterogenous between patients (ANCA-associated vasculitis-like disease, lupus or immune-complex mediated glomerulonephritis or overlapping phenotypes), and all had advanced histological damage at clinical presentation.

evidence.reference_title: COPA mutations impair ER-Golgi transport and cause hereditary autoimmune-mediated lung disease and arthritis.
evidence.snippet: We identified four unique deleterious variants in the COPA gene (encoding coatomer subunit α) affecting the same functional domain.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We described 3 heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain (CTD) of COPA (p.C1013S, p.R1058C, and p.R1142X) in 6 children from 3 unrelated families with a similar syndrome of autoinflammation and autoimmunity.

nodes.description: Heterozygous deleterious COPA variants - most commonly missense changes in the N-terminal WD40 cargo-recognition domain of coatomer subunit alpha, with a smaller subset in the C-terminal domain - initiate the molecular cascade underlying COPA syndrome. The disease behaves as a dominant interferonopathy with incomplete clinical penetrance.

evidence.reference_title: COPA mutations impair ER-Golgi transport and cause hereditary autoimmune-mediated lung disease and arthritis.
evidence.snippet: We identified four unique deleterious variants in the COPA gene (encoding coatomer subunit α) affecting the same functional domain.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We described 3 heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain (CTD) of COPA (p.C1013S, p.R1058C, and p.R1142X) in 6 children from 3 unrelated families with a similar syndrome of autoinflammation and autoimmunity.

nodes.description: Golgi-retained STING drives persistent cGAS/STING-dependent type I interferon pathway activation through TBK1-IRF3 signalling and downstream JAK-STAT amplification, producing the elevated IFN-alpha and interferon-stimulated gene (ISG) signature seen in symptomatic patients. JAK inhibition is the principal mechanism-targeted therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in COPA lead to abnormal trafficking of STING to the Golgi and interferon signaling.
evidence.snippet: We observed elevated levels of ISGs and IFN-α in blood of symptomatic COPA patients.

evidence.reference_title: A defect in COPI-mediated transport of STING causes immune dysregulation in COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Activated STING stimulates type I interferon-driven inflammation in CopaE241K/+ mice that is rescued in STING-deficient animals.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The disturbed intracellular trafficking resulted in a cGAS/STING-dependent upregulation of the type I IFN signaling in patients and patient-derived cell lines

nodes.description: Alveolar hemorrhage may be clinically overt or insidious and is a distinctive pulmonary complication of COPA syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Insights from a novel monogenic autoinflammatory disease: overview of a multicentric European cohort of 38 patients with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary involvement was observed in 34 patients, with interstitial lung disease in most cases (n = 31) and diffuse alveolar haemorrhage in 11 individuals.

nodes.description: Disturbed intracellular trafficking accumulates misfolded cargo and activates the unfolded protein response. ER stress contributes to proinflammatory cytokine output, NF-kappaB activation, and TH17 polarisation, and is genetically conserved in C. elegans copa-1 disease models.
nodes.id: ER stress and unfolded protein response

evidence.reference_title: COPA mutations impair ER-Golgi transport and cause hereditary autoimmune-mediated lung disease and arthritis.
evidence.snippet: expression of mutant COPA results in ER stress and the upregulation of cytokines priming for a T helper type 17 (TH17) response.

evidence.reference_title: copa-1 mutants experience heightened endoplasmic reticulum stress sensitivity in a C. elegans COPA Syndrome model.
evidence.snippet: these animals demonstrate an increased ER stress sensitivity.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We showed that CTD COPA mutations induce an activation of ER stress and NF-κB signaling in patient-derived primary cell lines.

nodes.description: Immune-mediated kidney disease occurs in a subset of patients and may present as ANCA-associated, lupus-like, or immune-complex glomerulonephritis.

evidence.reference_title: Kidney Transplant Outcomes in Coatomer Protein Complex Subunit Alpha (COPA) Syndrome: Report of Five Patients.
evidence.snippet: Kidney involvement was heterogenous between patients (ANCA-associated vasculitis-like disease, lupus or immune-complex mediated glomerulonephritis or overlapping phenotypes), and all had advanced histological damage at clinical presentation.

nodes.description: Mutant COPA fails to bind dilysine ER-retrieval motifs and the SURF4 adapter, blocking selection of STING and other cargo into COPI-coated vesicles at the cis-Golgi. CTD variants additionally destabilise the coatomer assembly itself, producing both anterograde and retrograde trafficking defects.

evidence.reference_title: COPA mutations impair ER-Golgi transport and cause hereditary autoimmune-mediated lung disease and arthritis.
evidence.snippet: COPA variants impair binding to proteins targeted for retrograde Golgi-to-ER transport.

evidence.reference_title: A defect in COPI-mediated transport of STING causes immune dysregulation in COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: SURF4 is an adapter molecule that facilitates COPA-mediated retrieval of STING at the Golgi.

nodes.description: Loss of COPI-mediated cargo selection compromises retrograde retrieval of client proteins, including STING, from the Golgi to the endoplasmic reticulum and perturbs ER-Golgi homeostasis. CTD variants also disrupt anterograde ER-to-Golgi transport.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: we demonstrated that COPI dysfunction causes both an anterograde ER-to-Golgi and a retrograde Golgi-to-ER trafficking defect.

nodes.description: Pulmonary disease is the dominant organ manifestation and is most often an interstitial lung disease pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Insights from a novel monogenic autoinflammatory disease: overview of a multicentric European cohort of 38 patients with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Pulmonary involvement was observed in 34 patients, with interstitial lung disease in most cases (n = 31) and diffuse alveolar haemorrhage in 11 individuals.

nodes.description: JAK inhibitors block JAK-STAT signalling downstream of the type I interferon receptor and are the most promising mechanism-targeted therapy in COPA syndrome. Evidence level: observational cohort and review-level support, with published cohort-level evidence supporting baricitinib and ruxolitinib for both interstitial lung disease and arthritis.

evidence.reference_title: Insights from a novel monogenic autoinflammatory disease: overview of a multicentric European cohort of 38 patients with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-two patients were treated with Janus kinase inhibitors with promising efficacy.

evidence.reference_title: COPA Syndrome-From Pathogenesis to Treatment.
evidence.snippet: JAK inhibitor therapy seems to be the most promising therapeutic choice now.

evidence.reference_title: Baricitinib treatment in children with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Baricitinib treatment in children with COPA syndrome.

nodes.description: Sustained ER stress and Golgi-localised STING signalling activate canonical NF-kappaB and induce IL-1beta, IL-6, and other proinflammatory cytokines that amplify autoinflammation. NF-kappaB activation is most prominently demonstrated in CTD variant cells.

evidence.reference_title: Heterozygous mutations in the C-terminal domain of COPA underlie a complex autoinflammatory syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CTD COPA mutations result in disease by increased ER stress, disturbed intracellular transport, and increased proinﬂammatory signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Genetics of COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Immunological features involve autoantibody formation, elevated expression of IL-1β and IL-6, and increase in the number of Th17 cells.

nodes.description: Rheumatoid factor is positive in a majority of patients and frequently leads to misdiagnosis as juvenile idiopathic arthritis or rheumatoid arthritis before genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: Analysis of pulmonary features and treatment approaches in the COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All subjects were positive for anti-neutrophil cytoplasmic antibody, anti-nuclear antibody or both and 71% of subjects had rheumatoid factor positivity.

nodes.description: Failed COPA/SURF4-mediated retrieval traps ER-resident STING at the Golgi, where it multimerises and undergoes ligand-independent activation with TBK1 phosphorylation. STING activation is constitutive in WD40 variants and hyperactivated upon stimulation in dendritic cells from CTD- and other mutant carriers.
nodes.id: STING accumulation and activation at the Golgi

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in COPA lead to abnormal trafficking of STING to the Golgi and interferon signaling.
evidence.snippet: mutant COPA was associated with an accumulation of ER-resident STING at the Golgi.

evidence.reference_title: A defect in COPI-mediated transport of STING causes immune dysregulation in COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We show that a defect in COPI transport causes ligand-independent activation of STING.

evidence.reference_title: Augmentation of Stimulator of Interferon Genes-Induced Type I Interferon Production in COPA Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In CopaV242G/+ dendritic cells, the STING pathway was not constitutively activated but was hyperactivated upon stimulation, leading to increased type I IFN production.

nodes.description: Direct STING pathway inhibition (e.g., small-molecule inhibitors such as H-151) and STING1-directed gene therapy aimed at introducing the protective HAQ allele are emerging upstream therapeutic strategies based on the central role of Golgi-localised STING activation in COPA syndrome. Evidence level: experimental/preclinical, with BI 3000202 now in Phase 1 safety and tolerability testing for selected type I interferonopathies including COPA syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: The common HAQ STING allele prevents clinical penetrance of COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Expressing HAQ STING in patient cells rescued the molecular phenotype of COPA syndrome.

nodes.description: Sustained type I interferon signalling, ER stress, and IL-6/IL-1beta output skew CD4+ T cells toward a TH17 fate, drive B-cell autoantibody production, and produce the autoimmune cytokine milieu underlying chronic injury in lung, joint, and kidney tissues.

evidence.reference_title: COPA mutations impair ER-Golgi transport and cause hereditary autoimmune-mediated lung disease and arthritis.
evidence.snippet: Patient-derived CD4(+) T cells also demonstrate significant skewing toward a TH17 phenotype that is implicated in autoimmunity.

evidence.reference_title: Insights from a novel monogenic autoinflammatory disease: overview of a multicentric European cohort of 38 patients with COPA syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All but 1 patient tested positive for autoantibodies, and increased interferon signalling was noted in all those tested.
